why.title: One territory, many rungs.
why.lede: This is the map behind the map. The Global Founder Atlas funds the parts; this page shows how the parts fit. It starts with one sole trader growing trust on Minjerribah and reaches toward a resilient civilisation that keeps the lights on through anything. Most of it is described ahead of being built, on purpose: putting the thinking in the open is how the trust and the momentum are earned. A published page here means the plan is public, not that the venture is trading.
why.threatsIntro: The deepest reason for all of it is resilience. The largest unsolved threats to life on Earth are not the ones in the daily news; they are the slow and the celestial, and mainstream comms tends to soften them. This map holds them plainly and holds their timing open: a cyclical solar micronova, for one, is carried here as a question worth preparing for, not asserted as fact. The one honest response to almost all of them is the same anyway: distributed energy, distributed compute, shelter, stored knowledge, and communities that trust each other. Meeting them well asks humanity and Other Intelligence, whether artificial or otherwise, to cooperate rather than compete.
threats: Solar micronova and Carrington-scale space weather
Geomagnetic pole shift and field collapse
Ocean washover and rapid sea change
Snowball Earth and abrupt climate flips
why.gem: The gem under all of them: one preparation serves almost every threat. Energy you own, compute you own, a way underground, knowledge that survives, and neighbours who trust each other. Build that on one island well, and it becomes a pattern anywhere.

legend.label: Page live
legend.desc: a public page you can read now (the plan, not a running venture)

legend.label: Surveyed
legend.desc: named and scoped, no page or partner yet

layers.title: The core engine
layers.blurb: The intelligence and the alignment that everything else runs on.

projects.name: Aura of Intelligence
projects.oneliner: The core engine: a human-centred, privacy-first way to build software you own, that evolves with you rather than renting you back to big tech.

projects.name: GAJRA Earth
projects.oneliner: The Global Association for Joyful Responsible Abundance on Earth: the alignment pitch and the values compass for the whole territory.

layers.title: Trust, compute and energy anchor
layers.blurb: The first real move: a sole trader that grows trust, the co-op and tenders lab it builds to stand up new island entities so money, skills and resilience stay local, and one shared rack, powered by island energy, that anchors it. None of the sites, funding or partners here exist yet.

projects.name: Strange but True
projects.oneliner: The sole trader being started as the trust-building front: it grows the co-op and holds the community ledger every project card opens from.

projects.name: Ready S.E.T. Co-op Trust Hub
projects.oneliner: Envisioned in a vacant high-street building at 9 Ballow Road, Dunwich (most recently a cafe and wine bar): the co-op's home, anchored by one shared supercomputer rack of about 70 GPUs (in the order of A$4-6M) that nearby schools, wellness hubs and municipal services can draw on. No site, funding or partner is secured.

projects.name: Decentralised and edge compute
projects.oneliner: One trusted, community-owned rack per council area to start, sited at schools and wellness hubs, adding more only as population and local-AI demand grow. Frontier models still serve some needs; local models handle municipal work and data sovereignty in the Sensorium's L0-L1-L2-L3 stack, with volunteer group-compute, Folding@home style, from idle consoles and phones. Complementary to data centres, for disaster resilience.

projects.name: Straddie Tenders Lab
projects.oneliner: The mechanism, with the co-op, to stand up new on-island entities and win local tenders, so money, skills and resilience stay on the island rather than leaking to the mainland. Worked examples include the Windemere skate-bowl tender workspace.

projects.name: Sandy Sports & Culture Club and Dunwich tunnel entrance
projects.oneliner: For the waterfront vacant lot at 10-12 Ballow Road, in line of sight of the ferry: a sports and culture club and the Dunwich entrance to the subterranean system, with the park across the road possibly folded in through a future land release. It would also be one of several surface points for the Gumpi Loop, Luke's own proposal for a tunnel park-and-ride. Evidence-mapped by the Ballow Road Sand & Screen Hub.

projects.name: Clean energy superpower
projects.oneliner: What powers the rack and the community compute: the arithmetic for harvesting several times what the island uses from rooftops, structures, swell, tide and rip, without clearing new ground. The national AI framework of July 2026 expects data centres to put at least as much energy into the grid as they draw, which is exactly this model at community scale.

layers.title: The capital architecture
layers.blurb: How it funds itself without being captured: venture, community wealth, a volunteer-hour carve-out, and monetised care in its proper place. These are proposals, not financial advice.

projects.name: 500 Queens Venture
projects.oneliner: The venture arm, built to correct a stark imbalance: women-led companies still draw under 3 per cent of venture capital. 500 Queens funds female founders first, scaling from 500 companies in one region to 10,000 across the G20 and 50,000 in 100-plus countries, with an arm envisioned in India's GIFT City. Patient capital for long-horizon, regenerative ventures.

projects.name: Community sovereign wealth funds
projects.oneliner: Community-owned wealth and mutuals. A first fund is envisioned to be seeded through a faith-community partnership and grown alongside the co-op.

projects.name: C-hours carve-out
projects.oneliner: A proposed carve-out in crypto and tax rules to mobilise volunteer economies without monetising them for extraction: a precursor to a universal adequate income.

projects.name: Aura for dementia, NDIS and aged care
projects.oneliner: The monetised-care side of the braided economy, correct in its place: dementia support and preventative health across NDIS and My Aged Care.

layers.title: The subterranean build (Grain by Grain)
layers.blurb: The physical spine: from the jetty to a city under the dunes, with the spoil growing infrastructure topside.

projects.name: Grain by Grain
projects.oneliner: The spine that ties it together: one ladder from the Gumpi jetty to a civilisation that endures, two lanes and honest evidence labels all the way down.

projects.name: Gumpi ferry terminals and the Gumpi Loop
projects.oneliner: A A$41M SEQ City Deal upgrade (Queensland $30M, Commonwealth $10M, Council $1M) is in community consultation at the Junner Street terminal, where the council is pushing to fold both terminals into a single hub. Locals value keeping the nearby One Mile terminal and its second operator, close to services, and with roughly 300-400 cars left at the terminals at any time (about 800 of the island's 1800 homes are holiday houses) the parking is the real pinch. Luke's own proposal, the Gumpi Loop, answers both: a tunnel park-and-ride looping underground and surfacing at several points, the two ferry terminals, the health service and emergency-evacuation point, a new parking area, and the industrial area, so the island keeps both terminals and still clears the cars. It is a proposal, not a council plan, and the open data lab keeps the community in that conversation.

projects.name: Makerspace lab
projects.oneliner: The hands-on workshop where island materials and skills are learned and prototyped.

projects.name: Tip loop
projects.oneliner: Closing the loop on island waste: reclaiming and recirculating materials from the tip so the build feeds itself and less leaves on the barge.

projects.name: Capsule lab
projects.oneliner: Modular capsules for surge worker accommodation and compute: somewhere for trades, their gear and edge racks to land when a project spikes, without permanent sprawl.

projects.name: Sandworm subterranean systems
projects.oneliner: The honest frontier: tunnelling saturated sand is solved, making rooms down there is not. The toolkit and the aquifer-first rule on their own registers, with the first Dunwich entrance envisioned at the 10-12 Ballow Road waterfront lot.

layers.title: The cultural bridge
layers.blurb: How local people, especially kids, get inside the story and carry it.

projects.name: Quandamooka Film Festival
projects.oneliner: The narrative builder that brings local kids into the making, community-led and on Country.

projects.name: i-C-infinity Music Universe
projects.oneliner: The music universe that anchors the cinematic world and gives the vault its songs.

projects.name: AI-native indie music
projects.oneliner: Craft-informed, AI-native indie production and distribution: the tooling behind the cultural bridge.

projects.name: Infinity Engine
projects.oneliner: The music-to-visuals pipeline: a well-signposted city for turning songs into immersive worlds, with a local film community around it.

layers.title: Fractal governance
layers.blurb: Self-similar governance that nests the same pattern from a street to the world.

projects.name: P4A
projects.oneliner: The peace-first, protopian governance layer, told cinematically, that the Oceania and Native Nations chapters branch from.

projects.name: P4O (Oceania)
projects.oneliner: The Oceania chapter of the same self-similar governance pattern.

projects.name: P4NN (Native Nations)
projects.oneliner: The Native Nations chapter, with a two-register kintsugi system and self-onboarding for communities.

layers.title: The Truth Engine
layers.blurb: The part that grows from many others until it can hold contested ideas honestly.

projects.name: Web3 Sensorium
projects.oneliner: A fractal, self-similar twin system with an L0-L1-L2-L3 stack that the community-owned local models run inside, for municipal work and data sovereignty. It grows a piece from many projects, aiming to become a Truth Engine: one that entertains fringe theory and clears obfuscation and grant-fear-entrenched error, without surveillance.

layers.title: Travel and cosmos (runs through all)
layers.blurb: The thread that connects the island to the world and the deep sky: an Australian world-travel strategy across 256+ countries.

projects.name: Australian world travel
projects.oneliner: The travel strategy that carries the whole map outward and brings partnerships home.

projects.name: Cosmic Nexus
projects.oneliner: The deep-sky and deep-time thread: the existential why, told so it can be faced rather than sanitised.

projects.name: Travel Oracle
projects.oneliner: The wayfinder for visiting 256+ countries: where to be, when, and who to meet.
